title: Vero Meets Kana In Atomweight Kickboxing Clash At The Inner Circle On May 22
description: The two stars will attempt to break into the ONE Women's Atomweight Kickboxing World Title picture.
fullText:
Two of the most exciting female strikers on the planet will step into the ring for a three-round war at The Inner Circle on Friday, May 22, and the winner could leave the Thai capital with the next shot at ONE World Title gold.

Former Lethwei Champion Vero “The Kayan Leopard” is scheduled to lock horns with former K-1 Champion Kana “Krusher Queen” Morimoto at the historic Lumpinee Stadium in Bangkok, Thailand.

Whoever emerges victorious could become the leading contender for a crack at “The Queen” Phetjeeja Lukjaoporongtom’s ONE Women’s Atomweight Kickboxing World Title.

After dropping a razor-close split decision in her promotional debut back in February 2025, Vero has only become stronger and more dangerous with every Muay Thai outing.

The 29-year-old Myanmar sensation returned to knock out Junior Fairtex in May 2025 and then she edged out Chinese striker Li “Miracle Girl” Mingrui via split decision six months later. She followed that up with another nail-biting victory, defeating Israel’s Shir Cohen in a closely-contested battle this past February.

Now confidently riding a three-bout winning streak with triumphs over world-class competition, “The Kayan Leopard” is making the switch to kickboxing and looking to earn her biggest victory yet in the world’s largest martial arts organization.

And beating a former ONE Women’s Atomweight Kickboxing World Title challenger would nicely position her for a crack at the coveted gold.

Kana is just as motivated to leave Lumpinee Stadium with her hand raised, however.

The Japanese veteran had a tough start to life in ONE Championship, falling to the legendary Anissa “C18” Meksen in December 2024. But the “Krusher Queen” shook off the loss and quickly returned to action, rattling off a unanimous decision victory over Swedish dynamo Moa “The X-Guard” Carlsson just a month later.

That outstanding performance led to a crack at Phetjeeja’s ONE Women’s Atomweight Kickboxing World Title in March 2025, and while it was a competitive five-round affair, the Thai left the ring with the belt.

Kana has been vocal about getting a World Title rematch against Phetjeeja ever since, but the Japanese veteran knows she needs to build some momentum toward that dream opportunity.

The 33-year-old got the ball rolling by spoiling former three-sport queen Stamp Fairtex’s return last November, outworking her en route to a unanimous decision victory.

If Kana can add Vero’s name to her list of victims and dazzle in the Thai capital on May 22, then she would be one step closer to her dream rematch.

Stay locked to onefc.com

title: Joey Browner was part of a special era in USC NFL draft history
description: The early 1980s were a great time for USC football in the NFL draft. Joey Browner was an important part of that larger story
fullText:
USC football's glory days included the late 1970s and the very early 1980s. Accordingly, the Trojans loaded up at the NFL draft in that era. Joey Browner, the defensive back who died earlier this year, was part of USC football's NFL draft dominance in the early 1980s. We wrote about Browner's career after his death:

"Browner played for the Trojans from 1979-1982. As a freshman, he was part of one of the most talented teams in college football history that featured the likes of Charles White, Marcus Allen, Ronnie Lott, and Anthony Muñoz. That USC team finished 11-0-1 and defeated Ohio State in the Rose Bowl.

"Browner would also help lead the Trojans to a Fiesta Bowl appearance following the 1981 season. As a senior in 1982, he earned all-Pac-10 honors at safety after converting from cornerback prior to the year."

Now, to the NFL draft piece of this story for Browner: When he was taken with the No. 19 pick in the 1983 NFL draft, Joey Browner ensured that USC football had a first-round pick in four consecutive NFL drafts. Anthony Munoz was the guy in 1980. 1981 had three NFL draft picks in the first round, with Ronnie Lott leading the way at No. 8. In 1982, Marcus Allen -- the 1981 Heisman Trophy winner -- went in the first round to the Raiders. Then Browner went to the Minnesota Vikings in the first round in 1983.

USC football and the NFL draft have been a winning and successful combination. Joey Browner helped USC extend a first-round NFL draft streak which is part of the Trojans' rich NFL legacy.

This article originally appeared on Trojans Wire: USC football star Joey Browner helped Trojans make NFL draft history

title: Packers among many teams to host OT Max Iheanachor on pre-draft visit
description: The Packers probably won't have a chance to draft Arizona State OT Max Iheanachor, but they still hosted him for a pre-draft visit.
fullText:
The Green Bay Packers were one of 12 teams to host Arizona State offensive tackle Max Iheanachor on a pre-draft visit, per NFL reporter Aaron Wilson.

Iheanachor, a three-year starter at Arizona State who measures 6-6 and 321 pounds, is ranked as the No. 30 overall player on the consensus big board. Dane Brugler of The Athletic ranks him as the No. 33 overall player, putting him firmly in the mix to be a first-round pick. The Packers don't come on the clock until No. 52 overall in the second round.

Iheanachor moved from Nigeria to the United States at age 13 and didn't start playing football until 2021, per Brugler. He is a raw but talented-filled prospect with a unique path to football, providing a likely reason why 12 teams wanted to host him pre-draft. Iheanachor also had labrum surgery before the 2025 season, so there would be medical reasons as well.

Iheanachor made 28 starts at right tackle at Arizona State. He was an All-Big 12 pick during his senior season in 2025, when he did not allow a sack across over 500 pass-blocking snaps. Iheanachor did commit nine penalties, including six penalties, so he remains a work in progress. But he steadily improved over his three seasons at Arizona State and his best football is almost certainly ahead of him.

At the combine, Iheanachor completed 25 reps on the bench and ran the 40-yard dash in 4.91 seconds at 321 pounds. He also hit 30.5" in the vertical and covered 9-7 in the broad jump. His Relative Athletic Score is 9.86 out of 10, with elite speed and elite explosive jumps.

Would the Packers consider Iheanachor if he fell into the second round? The Packers have Jordan Morgan expected to take over at left tackle and Zach Tom entrenched at right tackle, but Tom has multi-position versatility. It appears the Packers took measures to be prepared for the possibility of Iheanachor being available on Day 2.

This is the 28th reported visitor. Packers Wire is tracking all the team's visitors here.

This article originally appeared on Packers Wire: Packers among many teams to host OT Max Iheanachor on pre-draft visit

title: 76ers vs Celtics Prediction, Picks & Odds for NBA Playoffs Game 1
description: Another night of Jaylen Brown doing it all and delivering is to be expected in our Game 1 picks, odds & predictions for 76ers vs. Celtics for Sunday, April 19.
fullText:
Want to get more Covers content? Add us as a preferred source on your Google account here.

The Boston Celtics defeated the Philadelphia 76ers in seven games in the 2023 Eastern Conference playoffs, and the two teams will face off again when the 2026 first-round series tips off at TD Garden today.

Jaylen Brown has delivered an MVP-caliber season, and my 76ers vs. Celtics predictions expect the 2024 Finals MVP to come through with a strong performance in Game 1.

Read on to find out why I expect Brown to stuff the stat sheet in a comfortable home victory in my NBA picks for Sunday, April 19.

With Jayson Tatum sidelined for most of the season and key players from the Boston Celtics title run playing for new teams, Jaylen Brown asserted his dominance and delivered the best statistical campaign of his career. Brown averaged career highs in points (28.7), rebounds (6.9) and assists (5.1).

Brown finished with 37 PRA in 52 of 71 games overall, including 27 of 38 games at TD Garden. His production didn’t slip much with Tatum back in action.

Brown averaged 41.2 PRA without Tatum and 31.4 in 13 games with Tatum in the lineup. He accumulated 37+ PRA in nine of 13 games played with his superstar teammate.

Brown found success against the Philadelphia 76ers this season, averaging 38.6 PRA across four matchups. He delivered 37+ PRA twice and finished with 35 twice more.

The 76ers will be without defensive anchor Joel Embiid yet again, making this a favorable matchup for Brown. Philadelphia’s 116.5 defensive rating without Embiid would rank ninth-worst in the Association.

After a disappointing exit in the 2025 playoffs, the Celtics look primed for a deep postseason run, and their success will depend heavily on Brown’s leadership and performance.

After proving that he can be “the guy” for a contending team in the regular season, I expect Brown to carry that momentum and mindset into the playoffs and set the tone early for he and his teammates.

Philly and Boston split the four-game season series at two games apiece, but the Sixers have had a difficult time playing at TD Garden without Embiid.

In the last three games played in Boston without the big man, the 76ers were outscored by 17.3 points per game. Boston sports a +8.3 net rating at home this season, and with the team close to full strength, I’ll back them to win this one by 13+.

No team hit the Under at a higher percentage than the Boston Celtics at 63.4%. The C’s kept the score low throughout the season thanks to a bottom-5 pace and top-5 defense.

Without Embiid, the Sixers’ offensive rating dipped from 118.5 to 112.9, good for eighth-worst. Philly will have trouble scoring, and I expect Boston to make this one a slow grind.

Jaylen Brown Over 36.5 points + rebounds + assists

Re-united and it feels so good! Both Jaylen Brown and Jayson Tatum are in line for big games against a vulnerable defense as they look to lead their team to another title.

Jaylen Brown finished with 26+ points in 50 of 71 games overall, including nine of 13 with Jayson Tatum in the lineup. Tatum went for 23+ points in nine of 16 games and 24+ in six of 16.

Brown’s career-best year as a facilitator should continue into the playoffs. He handed out 5+ assists in 35 of 71 appearances, and he reached that mark in eight of 13 games played with Tatum.

Tatum’s rebounding is through the roof this season, as he averaged 10 boards across 16 regular-season appearances. Tatum secured 10+ boards in 10 of 16 games and seven of nine at TD Garden. Without Embiid to patrol the paint, I expect Tatum to gobble up rebounds.

Spread: Philadelphia +12.5 (-110) | Boston -12.5 (-110)

Moneyline: Philadelphia +550 | Boston -800

Over/Under: Over 213.5 (-110) | Under 213.5 (-110)

The Boston Celtics have hit the Game Total Under in 35 of their last 50 games (+18.50 Units / 34% ROI). Find more NBA betting trends for 76ers vs. Celtics.

title: Karl Anthony Towns has hilarious postgame reaction to Game of Thrones star
description: Madison Square Garden pulls A-list celebrities courtside on any given night, but Saturday brought a different kind of moment for Karl-Anthony Towns. The New York Knicks’ big man put up 25 points and eight rebounds in a 113-102 win over…
fullText:
Madison Square Garden pulls A-list celebrities courtside on any given night, but Saturday brought a different kind of moment for Karl-Anthony Towns.

The New York Knicks’ big man put up 25 points and eight rebounds in a 113-102 win over the Atlanta Hawks in Game 1 of their Eastern Conference NBA playoff series. What came afterward is what went viral.

MORE: LeBron James names craziest moment of his NBA career after Lakers playoff win

Towns ran into Kit Harington postgame. The actor played Jon Snow on “Game of Thrones,” and he showed up to watch the Knicks grab a 1-0 series lead.

When the two crossed paths, Towns dropped a line that would make any Westeros fan proud.

“I would bend the knee, but my knee hurts right now,” Towns told Harington, via NBA on Prime.

The Knicks star mentioned he appreciated Harington coming to the game. Towns also shared that he and his wife, Jordyn Woods, recently started rewatching “Game of Thrones” together.

Towns watched the series when it originally aired, but he’s going through it again alongside Woods. The two posed for a photo, and the mutual respect between the actor and the Knicks center was clear.

KAT 🤝 King of the North

Towns was so hyped meeting Kit Harington postgame at the Garden 🐺 pic.twitter.com/u50yc2Equn

— NBA on Prime (@NBAonPrime) April 19, 2026

MORE: NBA playoffs map shows every state’s pick to win 2026 Finals

The series stays at Madison Square Garden for Game 2 on Tuesday, and the energy inside the arena won’t drop off.

For Towns, the break comes at a good time. He gets a few days to manage that knee soreness he joked about with Harington before stepping back on the floor.

The Knicks need him healthy if they want to keep pushing in the playoffs, and a little rest between games could make the difference as the series continues.

title: Brotherhood Playoff News & Links
description: Luke Kennard really came through for JJ Redick and the Lakers
fullText:

title: Jets sign 27-year old forward to $2.9 million contract
description: Winnipeg is staying busy even at the end of the season.
fullText:
Jets sign 27-year old forward to $2.9 million contract originally appeared on The Sporting News. Add The Sporting News as a Preferred Source by clicking here.

The Winnipeg Jets' season is over after missing the playoffs.

They aren't resting on their laurels, though. They've already completed some offseason business.

On Saturday, it was announced that Cole Koepke has signed a contract extension with the Jets.

The Athletic's Chris Johnston reported that it's a two-year deal worth $2.9 million in total value ($1.45 million per year).

MORE: This Connor Hellebuyck trade rumor should scare the rest of the NHL

Koepke is a 27-year old forward who had originally signed with the Jets before the 2025-26 season on a one-year contract for $1 million.

He then played 66 games for Winnipeg, recording 17 total points that included eight goals and nine assists.

Koepke has done well for himself as originally a sixth-round pick by the Tampa Bay Lightning in 2018. He spent his time after being drafted playing college hockey at Minnesota-Duluth.

He spent a couple NHL seasons with the Lightning, although he was mostly down in the AHL in Syracuse.

Before the 2024-25 season, Koepke joined the Boston Bruins and recorded 17 points (10 goals, seven assists).

MORE: Porter Martone makes the most of hockey's unique timeline

That brought him to Winnipeg, and apparently the partnership worked well enough that they wanted to bring him back.

The Jets still have Gustav Nyquist as an unrestricted free agent forward, and they're waiting on Jonathan Toews' retirement decision before they move ahead with further potential moves to add firepower to their offensive ranks.

fullText:
TalkSPORT host Andy Goldstein admitted he feels sorry for Chelsea fans because the club’s owners don’t care about winning.

DOWNLOAD THE OFFICIAL CHELSEA NEWS APP FOR ALL THE LATEST UPDATES – STRAIGHT TO YOUR PHONE! ON THE APP STORE

Chelsea slumped to their fourth consecutive league defeat without scoring as they lost 1-0 to Manchester United on Saturday evening.

The Blues have only won one of their last seven games, which was against League One side Port Vale in the FA Cup.

Before the game Chelsea and Strasbourg fans united to protest against BlueCo, with thousands lining the streets to voice their dissatisfaction.

The Todd Boehly and Clearlake Capital ownership inherited a Champions League winning squad, but Chelsea couldn’t be anymore unrecognisable at this point.

The Blues are staring one Champions League qualification in four years right in the face, and Joe Cole has warned players will want to leave.

Co-owner Behdad Eghbali has said the model will be tweaked, but fans rightly won’t believe that until they see it.

Chelsea fans are used to success and winning, and talkSPORT host Goldstein believes the owners only care about the business model.

“Chelsea used to have an owner in Roman Abramovich who only cared about football and winning,” he told talkSPORT as quoted on X.com.

“Now they’ve got owners who only care about the business model and whether or not the business makes money. This is why I feel sorry for the fans.”

title: Georgia football trying to flip 4-star Texas commit
description: The Georgia Bulldogs are the latest elite college football program to try to pry four-star defensive back Karnell James away from Texas
fullText:
The Georgia Bulldogs have made move for four-star Texas Longhorns commitment Karnell "Greedy" James. The four-star defensive back recruit is a member of the class of 2027 and has been committed to Texas since Dec. 2025.

Georgia offered James a scholarship on April 15. The 6-foot, 175-pound defensive back is one of Texas' eight commitments in the class of 2027. The Longhorns have the nation's No. 12 recruiting class. James is Texas' only defensive back commitment.

James plays high school football for Manvell High School in Manvell, Texas. The four-star is ranked as the No. 26 safety recruit in the country and the No. 43 prospect in Texas, per 247Sports. In the 247Sports Composite, James is the nation's No. 473 recruit. He was recently promoted to a four-star ranking.

On film, James immediately stands out as a dynamic punt returner. He's capable of taking any punt to the house. The Manvell star has a knack for being around the football and forcing turnovers. James is a ballhawk that primarily plays safety. He projects as a deep safety at the next level, but he's more than capable of tackling in open space. It is easy to see why so many top college football programs are interested in James.

The elite defensive back prospect has recently visited Notre Dame, so it appears that James is keeping his options open. Georgia will try to get him on campus to further sway the Texas commitment.

"Blessed to receive an offer from the University of Georgia," James said via social media. The Texas commitment has a 6-foot-6 wingspan and has a lot of recruiting momentum over the past few months. James has recent scholarship offers from Mississippi State, Kentucky, LSU, Notre Dame and Georgia.

title: A top cornerback draft prospect, All-American meets with Jaguars
description: The Jaguars have reportedly met with one of the top cornerback prospects in the 2026 NFL draft.
fullText:
The Jacksonville Jaguars have taken the time to meet with one of the top cornerback prospects in the 2026 NFL draft.

According to NFL insider Aaron Wilson, the Jaguars are one of several teams to meet virtually wth San Diego State cornerback Chris Johnson.

Let's take a look at what Johnson could add to the Jaguars' defense.

Johnson has started the last two seasons, with most of his snaps coming lined up outside. This past season, in particular, Johnson was very stingy in coverage, allowing a completion rate of just 42% and only 10.3 yards per catch. He had good ball production as well, totaling four interceptions and five pass breakups. Johnson was named a second-team All-American.

Johnson is the 38th-ranked prospect on the consensus big board and the fifth-ranked cornerback in this year's class. Many mock drafts have him being taken in the first round.

Chris Johnson is a CB prospect in the 2026 draft class. He scored a 9.82 RAS out of a possible 10.00. This ranked 52 out of 2822 CB from 1987 to 2026.https://t.co/u1NM1dInPy pic.twitter.com/IptDkKgXSj

— RAS.football (@MathBomb) March 18, 2026

For a closer look at Johnson's game, here is what NFL.com's Lance Zierlein had to say in his pre-draft scouting report:

"Scheme-versatile cornerback prospect with average size and the ability to play inside or outside. Johnson is consistent in mirroring the release from his pedal and rarely labors when transitioning with route breaks. While many college corners lean on lax officiating to grab and maul, Johnson stays disciplined. He trusts his footwork and technique to remain tight to the route. He attacks the catch point with run-through force and is a tenacious wrap-up tackler who can quickly end plays. Big receivers can steal some high-points, but not enough to cause concern. Johnson’s coverage versatility, toughness and athleticism should make him an early starter for a coverage-hungry team."

Adding Montaric Brown back into the mix greatly reduced the Jaguars' need to address the cornerback position. He and Travis Hunter will likely start on the outside, Jourdan Lewis will be at the nickel, while Jarrian Jones provides experienced depth. I would guess we still see James Gladstone add to this position in the draft, but no longer does doing so right away feel like a must.

This article originally appeared on Jaguars Wire: 2026 NFL draft: Jaguars meet with All-American CB Chris Johnson

title: Atalanta Join Roma In Race For Versatile Inter Milan & Brazil Star But Nerazzurri Stand Firm
description: Atalanta have entered the race to sign Carlos Augusto, joining Roma in pursuing the Inter Milan and Brazil defender as the versatile wing-back’s future at the club grows increasingly uncertain.Carlo...
fullText:
Atalanta have entered the race to sign Carlos Augusto, joining Roma in pursuing the Inter Milan and Brazil defender as the versatile wing-back’s future at the club grows increasingly uncertain.

Carlos Augusto covers two positions, left wing-back and left centre-back in a back three, making him one of the most attractive profiles available in the summer market.

According to L’Interista, Inter value him at a minimum of €25 million and will not open negotiations below that figure.

The defender’s hesitation over his Inter renewal stems not from money but from a desire for guaranteed starts.

Indeed, Dimarco is untouchable on the left flank, and the competition for places makes regular football easier to find elsewhere.

Furthermore, Chivu has been experimenting with Luis Henrique on the left in recent matches, a possible signal that the club are already planning for a future without Carlos Augusto.

Therefore, should Atalanta arrive with a formal offer, Inter could immediately move for Marco Palestra, their primary target for that position, in a deal that would cost 

However, that chain of events also depends on Dumfries.

If the Dutchman leaves for England, with Manchester United and Aston Villa monitoring his situation, the right flank opens up and the entire summer picture changes.

title: Reports suggest Serge Gnabry’s injury will sideline attacker through 2026 World Cup
description: Sport Bild reports that Bayern Munich attacker Serge Gnabry’s injury is sufficiently serious to sideline the 30-year-old through the World Cup. Yesterday, the German record champions revealed that G...
fullText:
Sport Bild reports that Bayern Munich attacker Serge Gnabry’s injury is sufficiently serious to sideline the 30-year-old through the World Cup. Yesterday, the German record champions revealed that Gnabry incurred an adductor injury during Wednesday’s Champions League victory. The severity of Gnabry’s thigh issues have not yet officially been confirmed by the Bundesliga outfit.

The German tabloid nevertheless claims that Gnabry has suffered an adductor tear that will keep him out of action for three months. This translates to the end of the season and the World Cup for the resurgent German international. Bundestrainer Julian Nagelsmann finds himself in need of another player to fill the No. 10 slot in his 4-2-3-1 formation.

Despite the fact that the 23-year-old did not feature under Nagelsmann during the recent March international break, Musiala has declared himself fit and ready travel to the United States to participate in the tournament.

Nagelsmann himself – as quoted in the Sport Bild article – has stated that “all players, including Jamal, must be 100 percent fit” in order to earn nomination for his preliminary squad to be released on May 12th.

title: Did Dalton Knecht play in Lakers-Rockets Game 1?
description: Former Vol does not play in Game 1 of NBA playoffs first round.
fullText:
No. 4 seed Los Angeles defeated No. 5 seed Houston, 107-98, Saturday in Game 1 of the NBA playoffs first round at Crypto.com Arena in Los Angeles, California.

Former Tennessee basketball player Dalton Knecht did not play for the Lakers due to a coach's decision.

Luke Kennard, a trade deadline acquisition, scored 27 points for the Lakers. LeBron James recorded a double-double, totaling 19 points, 13 assists and eight rebounds. Deandre Ayton also scored 19 points for Los Angeles. Austin Reaves and Luka Doncic were inactive.

Knecht was selected in the first round of the 2024 NBA draft (No. 17) by the Lakers.

He transferred to Tennessee from Northern Colorado and played for the Vols during the 2023-24 season under head coach Rick Barnes.

During his one season at Tennessee, Knecht averaged 21.7 points, 4.9 rebounds and 1.8 assists per game. He led the Vols to a SEC regular-season championship and an appearance in the NCAA Tournament Elite Eight for a second time in school history.

Los Angeles will next play Tuesday in Game 2 of the best-of-seven series. Tipoff is slated for 10:30 p.m. EDT at Crypto.com Arena (NBC).

Follow Vols Wire on Facebook and X (formerly Twitter).

title: Inter Club Workshop, a special day
description: A morning steeped in Inter colours saw the Inter Club network celebrated. On Sunday 19 April, in Milano hosted a workshop for club presidents and board members. The event brought together 350 represen...
fullText:
A morning steeped in Inter colours saw the Inter Club network celebrated. On Sunday 19 April, in Milano hosted a workshop for club presidents and board members. The event brought together 350 representatives from around the world, including members from 20 Inter Clubs within the Inter Club Coordination Centre.

The day began with the presentation of the outstanding results from the 2025/26 season. The Inter Club network reached impressive figures, with 203,673 members and 1,082 clubs worldwide, spanning 73 countries and including over 25,000 members outside Italy. These are significant milestones for Inter Clubs, which have now exceeded 200,000 members for the fourth consecutive season.

The meeting continued with a focus on future projects: attendees were presented with the new initiatives planned for the 2026/27 season, along with a dedicated segment on ticketing for Inter Club members.

The Inter Club Workshop was attended by President and CEO Giuseppe Marotta, who addressed those present and once again highlighted the central importance of the Inter Club network within the Nerazzurri family:

The day concluded with the presentation of the 2026 Inter Club Awards, recognising clubs that stood out for special achievements over the course of the season:

Biggest Membership Club, awarded to the largest club: INTER CLUB ALBANIA

Youngest Membership Club, for the club with the highest proportion of young members: INTER CLUB PEGASO NICOLA BERTI

Best Digitalised Club, awarded to the club with the highest proportion of members with digital profiles registered in the Inter Loyalty programme: INTER CLUB MODENA INTERISTA

Most Inclusive Club: INTER CLUB GRUPPO SORDI INTERISTI, made up predominantly of deaf members

Charity Hero: INTER CLUB FACCHETTI OF NEW JERSEY, a club long involved in organising a charity gala dinner in New York

Lifetime Achievement: SERGIO BERTOLDI, who has been part of the Inter Club Cornaredo board for 40 years, also serving as a steward and collaborating with the coordination centre

The Workshop concluded with a special and emotional moment, as President and CEO Giuseppe Marotta was joined by Vice President Javier Zanetti and Francesco Toldo for a dedicated photo and autograph session with the 350 presidents and board members in attendance. A special meet and greet, where Inter Club representatives were able to share their Nerazzurri passion with the two legends.

An unforgettable day celebrating Inter Clubs, one of the true beating hearts of the Nerazzurri world.

title: Shreyas Iyer to Mohammed Shami: 5 players to watch in today's IPL 2026 match between PBKS and LSG
description: Punjab Kings meet Lucknow Super Giants today in IPL 2026. Punjab Kings are the only unbeaten team. Lucknow Super Giants are struggling. Key player battles will decide the match. Prabhsimran Singh and Shreyas Iyer are in form for Punjab.
fullText:
Punjab Kings will take on Lucknow Super Giants in Match 29 of IPL 2026 at Mullanpur on April 19.

PBKS head into the clash as the only unbeaten team this season, topping the table with four wins in five matches. In contrast, LSG are struggling in seventh place, having lost three of their five games.

LSG face a concern with captain Rishabh Pant doubtful due to an elbow injury. Meanwhile, PBKS will rely on in-form Prabhsimran Singh (211 runs) and skipper Shreyas Iyer, who is enjoying exceptional form, while LSG will look to Prince Yadav (9 wickets) for early breakthroughs.

Here are the 5 key battles which can shape today's contest:

The contest between Mitchell Marsh and Arshdeep Singh has been a high-stakes powerplay duel in T20 cricket, with both players enjoying moments of dominance. Arshdeep has dismissed Marsh three times in T20s, with Marsh averaging just 15 against him. Despite this, Marsh has maintained an aggressive approach, scoring 45 runs off Arshdeep at a strike rate of 166.66.

The battle between Shreyas Iyer and Prince Yadav pits consistency against emerging pace. In their limited head-to-head meetings, Iyer has dominated, scoring 14 runs off just 6 balls at a strike rate of 233.3, including a four and a six. Iyer has been in red-hot form this season, scoring 203 runs in five matches at a strike rate of 187.96, averaging over 50 with three half-centuries. Meanwhile, Yadav has emerged as LSG’s primary wicket-taking option, especially in the powerplay, claiming 9 wickets in five matches at an average of 18.22, including a match-winning 3/32 against RCB.

The matchup between Rishabh Pant and Yuzvendra Chahal is a tactical battle between aggression and guile. Historically, Chahal has had the upper hand dismissing Pant 3 times conceding 92 runs in 64 balls. Despite being dismissed three times, Pant has maintained an attacking approach, making this a high-risk, high-reward contest.

The opening battle between Prabhsimran Singh and Mohammed Shami is a clash between form and experience. Prabhsimran has dominated this matchup historically, scoring 197 runs and being dismissed only once in T20 encounters. He enters this game in outstanding form, with 211 runs in four innings at an average of 70.33, including a match-winning 80* off 39 balls against Mumbai Indians. Shami, meanwhile, has made a strong comeback from injury, picking up 4 wickets in five matches with an impressive economy rate of 6.84, leading LSG’s powerplay attack.

The battle between Nicholas Pooran and Marco Jansen is crucial in the middle-to-death overs. Jansen has managed to keep Pooran relatively in check without dismissing him in IPL encounters. Nicholas Pooran has scored 66 runs in 42 balls and yet to get dismissed by him. While Pooran scores at a healthy strike rate, Jansen’s ability to build pressure through dot balls gives him a slight tactical edge.

title: Sycamores take three of four titles at Gibson Invitational track meet
description: The three-day Gibson Invitational meet concluded Saturday at the Gibson Track and Field Complex, with Indiana State taking three of the four team titles — Men's Collegiate, Women's Collegiate and Women's Invitational. Notre Dame finished atop the Men's Invitational team standings. Rose-Hulman also competed in this meet. ISU's Aliseonna Garnett finished second in the Women's Collegiate hammer ...
fullText:
The three-day Gibson Invitational meet concluded Saturday at the Gibson Track and Field Complex, with Indiana State taking three of the four team titles — Men's Collegiate, Women's Collegiate and Women's Invitational.

Notre Dame finished atop the Men's Invitational team standings. Rose-Hulman also competed in this meet.

ISU's Aliseonna Garnett finished second in the Women's Collegiate hammer throw with a distance of 54.54 meters and second in the Women's Invitational shot put with a 15.42, while teammates Emma Yoder (61.35) and Cora Williams (58.81) finished second and third respectively in the Women's Invitational hammer throw.

In the Men's Collegiate hammer throw, the Sycamores' Theo Thurmond finished second with a 56.5.

In the Women's Collegiate 5,000m run, Robyn Schemel finished second with a time of 18 minutes, 28.94 seconds.

Rachel Mehringer took a first-place finish in the Women's Collegiate 100 hurdles with a time of 13.10.

"She has been training really well," ISU program director and head track coach Angie Martin said of Mehringer. "She has a really big goal to try and run under 13 and last year at this time of year we were averaging about 13.4, 13.5, now we're averaging like 13.1."

Martin said Mehringer has the coaching staff really excited. However, she said the hard part is with her running so well, it's hard to find competition for her.

Martin said they lined her up with the guys Friday because they run the 110 hurdles and that pretty much equates to what she's running for the 100 hurdles.

"The guys get excited because they want to race her," Martin told the Tribune-Star. "They don't want to get beat by her, but then she has people kind of challenge her."

Martin said she has teammates pushing her as well.

Martin said they had some people trying some new things this weekend.

The male hurdlers recorded first and second-place finishes with Kieran Barnewall posting a time of 14.02 and Parke Doyle recording 14.07.

In the 100-meter dash, Janiya Bowman took third in the Women's Collegiate category (11.72). Terrance O'Bannon finished third for the men with a time of 10.44. Halle Miller tagged a time of 4:38.38 in the 1,500 run for a second-place finish.

The men recorded a second-place finish in the 4x100 relay with a time of 40.12.

Hallie Mimbela finished first in the women's 800 with a time of 2:16.37.

Trent Jones took a first-place finish in the men's 400 with a time of 47.18.

David Carnell finished second in the men's 400 hurdles with a time of 53.66.

Bowman finished first in the Women's Collegiate 200 with a time of 23.63.

In the women's 4x400 relay, ISU finished third with a time of 4:04.53.

In the Women's Collegiate discus, Yoder finished first with a distance of 47.45.

Ben Brown finished 11th in the men's discus collegiate with a distance of 44.96.

Noah Gilmore recorded a first-place finish in the high jump with a distance of 2.06.

In the women's pole vault, Amy Luttrell finished second with a distance of 3.89 with Campbell Roberts tying for third with a distance of 3.74. For the men, Lincoln Hulsey finished third with a distance of 4.96.

"I think both the men and the women pole vaulters are continuing to make progress each week," said ISU associate head coach Jeff Martin, who happens to be Angie's husband. "We identified some things during the indoor season we needed to work on and we've been making some changes during practices and it's starting to show during the competitions."

He said it's tough right now with school wrapping up with the stress that brings with finals coming along. Then there's the simple fact of being a Division I athlete that he mentioned.

The pole vault and high jump got moved inside, which Jeff Martin said helped them.

"They're all competitive and they all want to do well and when you have highly motivated individuals and with good competition, typically it brings out the best of them and good performances," Mr. Martin said.

Angie said she felt they let the weather get to them a little bit in the team's previous home meet.

"It got to some of us today, but not as bad," she added. "So that helped with some performances."

Angie Martin said her athletes understood her expectations with how she wanted them to compete.

The Sycamores will head to the Drake Relays in Des Moines, Iowa, on Wednesday with the event running through Saturday.

Angie said they're excited going into that noting they're going to do a couple of individual events such as the hurdles and a couple 10Ks.

"But I'm really excited to do some of the relays," Martin said. "The shuttle hurdles are going to be really fun because we're deep on both sides."

She said they're also going to run a distance medley relay for both the men and the women. They'll run a 4x800 on the men's side.

"We have some big goals to go place high at Drake and see if we can come home with maybe a Drake Relays flag or two," Martin said.

title: Inter Milan Learn Price Tag For Parma Star As Speculation Grows Over Summer Transfer
description: Inter Milan have identified Parma player Mandela Keita as a midfield target for the summer and must pay at least €20 million to secure the Belgium international, who has established himself as one o...
fullText:
Inter Milan have identified Parma player Mandela Keita as a midfield target for the summer and must pay at least €20 million to secure the Belgium international, who has established himself as one of Serie A’s most impressive defensive midfielders this season.

According to L’Interista, no formal dialogue has yet begun between the clubs, but Inter’s interest in Keita is genuine as they plan a significant overhaul of the midfield department.

The numbers behind Keita’s most recent performance against Napoli underline his appeal, six defensive contributions, four recoveries, four interceptions and two defensive clearances in a single match.

Indeed, Chivu’s planned switch toward a 3-4-2-1 requires exactly the profile Keita offers, a ball-winning, press-resistant midfielder capable of disrupting opponents and protecting the defence.

Furthermore, the Belgian could form a complementary partnership with Aleksandar Stankovic.

Indeed, whose return from Club Brugge is all-but confirmed with Inter set to trigger the €23 million buy-back clause.

Therefore, the midfield blueprint is beginning to take shape, a combination of Stankovic’s dynamism and Keita’s defensive intensity giving Chivu a very different engine room to the one that has powered Inter’s Scudetto campaign.

Parma will not sell cheaply. Their leverage is Keita’s outstanding form, and they intend to use it.

title: Private credit not only won’t spark a financial crisis — it may be more stable than your bank
description: With 65% equity cushions and 10-year lockups, Wall Street’s ‘anti-banks’ won’t face a ‘Lehman moment.’
fullText: Rising redemption pressures in parts of the private-credit market — and growing concern among regulators and commentators — have sharpened the debate over financial stability. As private credit has expanded rapidly over the past decade, so has a familiar question: Is this simply banking risk in another form? And could stress in private-credit funds spill over into the broader financial system?The concern is understandable. Financial crises are rarely caused by asset losses alone. Trouble begins when losses collide with fragile funding structures. Traditional banks, for instance, fund long-term, illiquid assets with short-term, runnable liabilities. When confidence falters, that mismatch can turn manageable losses into systemic crises. The key question, then, is whether private credit reproduces the balance-sheet features that make banks fragile.

title: Claude-codex-proxy – Use Claude Code with ChatGPT subscription
description: Use Claude Code with your ChatGPT subscription via a local Anthropic-compatible proxy - raine/claude-codex-proxy
fullText:
claude-codex-proxy lets you use
Claude Code with your ChatGPT Plus or
Pro subscription.

Quick start · How it works ·
Configuration · Limitations

I feel Claude Code is still the best harness around, despite occasional
frustrations caused by updates. However, Anthropic keeps tightening the usage
limits, while OpenAI is still much more generous.

If you want to use OpenAI plans, your best options seem to be OpenCode and
Codex. I tried OpenCode, but the UX has many rough edges, especially around
skills feeling like a second-class feature. Fortunately it's open source and I
ended up forking it and applying some patches, but would much rather not do it.

Install script (macOS and Linux):

Manual: download a prebuilt binary for your platform from the
releases page.

Or, on a headless machine (device code flow):

Either command prints a URL. Sign in with your ChatGPT Plus/Pro account. On
macOS, credentials are stored in Keychain. On other platforms, they are stored
locally for reuse by the proxy.

Defaults to http://127.0.0.1:18765 (loopback only). Override with
PORT=11435 claude-codex-proxy serve.

Or set it persistently in ~/.claude/settings.json:

Set ANTHROPIC_MODEL to a model your ChatGPT subscription is allowed to use.
Confirmed working on Plus:

Also verified working on this project:

The proxy also accepts a small set of convenience aliases and resolves them
before calling the upstream Codex backend:

These aliases are only shorthand for portability and cheaper subagent configs;
they are not semantic equivalents of the Claude models they resemble.

If the resolved model isn't supported by your account, upstream returns a 400
like
"The 'gpt-4.1' model is not supported when using Codex with a ChatGPT account.".
The proxy surfaces that verbatim.

For example, you can now point Claude Code or a subagent at the proxy with:

and the proxy will send gpt-5.4-mini upstream.

Starts the HTTP proxy and blocks. Binds to 127.0.0.1 only. Logs to
$XDG_STATE_HOME/claude-codex-proxy/proxy.log (rotated at 20 MiB). Set
CCP_LOG_STDERR=1 to mirror log lines to stderr while running.

Prints the exact ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL / ANTHROPIC_MODEL env vars to export on
startup. Refuses to start traffic until auth login (or auth device) has
stored a token.

Runs the PKCE browser flow against auth.openai.com using the Codex CLI's
client ID. Prints a URL, opens a local callback listener on port 1455, waits for
the browser to redirect back, and stores the resulting access / refresh tokens
in Keychain on macOS or locally on other platforms. The process exits
automatically once the tokens are saved.

Sign in with your ChatGPT Plus/Pro account, not an OpenAI API account. The
token file includes the extracted chatgpt_account_id so the proxy can set the
ChatGPT-Account-Id header on every upstream call.

Same OAuth flow, but for headless machines. Prints a short user code and a URL;
you enter the code from any browser on any other device, and the CLI polls
auth.openai.com until you authorize, then stores the token.

Useful over SSH, inside a container, or on any host that can't open a browser.

Shows whether credentials are stored, the account ID, and how long until the
access token expires. Non-zero exit if no auth is present.

The proxy refreshes the access token 5 minutes before expiry with a
single-flight guard, so concurrent requests never trigger stampedes of refresh
calls.

Removes stored auth credentials. On macOS this deletes the Keychain entry. No
server call is needed; the refresh token just becomes dead.

Run auth login again to re-authenticate.

The proxy speaks enough of the Anthropic API for Claude Code:

Settings are environment variables on the proxy process, not a config file.

Install a compiled dev build globally: compile the current working tree to a
binary and place it on your PATH without linking:

title: Atlassian's new data collection policy protects rich customers while AI eats
description: : From August 17, the outfit will collect customer metadata by default unless you pay for the top tier
fullText:
Unless a customer pays for the most expensive enterprise license, or the law forbids it, Atlassian is going to collect their data to train its AI models. And you can't fully opt out.

Beginning in August, the company will seek to collect two types of data from its 300,000 global customers: metadata and in-app data from Jira, Confluence, and its other cloud products, which will then be fed into the company's models.

Metadata includes readability scores and complexity ratings for Confluence page content, task classifications assigned to content (such as "sales work item"), semantic similarity scores measuring how similar two Confluence pages are, and numbers entered into Atlassian-created fields – specifically story points assigned to a Jira work item, the end date of a sprint in Jira, and the Service Level Agreement of a Jira Service Management request.

For the metadata collection, lower-paying customers are on the hook no matter what. “If an Atlassian customer's highest active plan is Free, Standard, or Premium, metadata contribution is always on, and they are not able to opt out,” Arseny Tseytlin, head of product communications at Atlassian, told The Register via email. “All metadata is de-identified and aggregated before it is used to improve apps and experiences for all customers. We remove information that directly identifies individuals, such as name and email addresses.”

Once it collects the information, Atlassian says it will store it for up to seven years.

“Retaining this data, which has been de-identified and aggregated at a customer level and is common across customers, enables us to make more meaningful observations over longer periods of time,” Tseytlin said. “By unlocking deeper insights into customer behavior, we’re able to drive continual improvements to (the) overall experience.”

Atlassian said that the training will give its apps the ability to surface the most relevant results in response to prompts and queries, summarize relevant content more accurately and concisely, identify the best templates for creating new documents, and learn which agentic workflows and follow-up questions lead to successful completions, making multi-step tasks faster and less confusing.

In-app data would be the content created by users within the Atlassian platform such as titles and content in Confluence pages, titles, descriptions, and comments in Jira work items, custom emoji names, custom Jira or Confluence status names, and custom workflow names. The Free and Standard tiers have this data collection turned on by default but can opt out; Premium and Enterprise customers have it turned off by default.

Tseytlin said that some Atlassian customers are completely excluded from metadata or in‑app "data contribution" entirely. This includes those who use customer-managed keys, or bring your own key, Atlassian Government Cloud, or Atlassian Isolated Cloud users. He said Atlassian will also not collect metadata or in-app data from customers with HIPAA compliance requirements or from some government and financial services customers.

The plan goes into effect on August 17.

“If customers were to right now terminate their contract, the new data contribution settings will not apply to them as these will not be enforced until August 17, 2026,” Tseytlin said.

After that date, once a customer opts out of having their data collected, or deletes their Atlassian apps, Atlassian will remove the corresponding in-app data from its datasets within 30 days. He said that, within 90 days, Atlassian will re-train any models previously trained on that data. ®
